rationale: The citing paper uses Sen and Bhattacharya (2001) in its conceptual development section to support a specific CSR premise: consumer beliefs and firm-CSR incongruity can shape evaluations and harm outcomes. This citation is tied to hypothesis framing about when firm green requests backfire, not just a generic background list. Although it is cited alongside another study, it meaningfully informs the paper's theoretical positioning on contingent consumer reactions to CSR signals.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Sen and Bhattacharya (2001) as prior evidence that CSR effects are contingent on consumer beliefs and firm characteristics, and uses that logic to motivate its own model of when environmental appeals by firms help or backfire. It builds its framing of incongruity-based negative responses on this earlier CSR consumer-response literature.
